turns.name: JUSTICE ALITO
turns.text: The second case is Brown versus The United States, which is consolidated with Jackson versus The United States 22-6640. The Armed Career Criminal Act imposes a 15-year mandatory minimum sentence on defendants who were convicted for illegally possessing a firearm and have a criminal history that demonstrates propensity for violence. Defendants are subject to this enhanced penalty if they have three previous convictions for a serious drug offense. These two cases present the question whether a state crime constitutes a serious drug offense if it involved a drug that was on the federal schedule when the defendant possessed or trafficked in it but was later removed. We hold that such an offense qualifies. The opinion of the Court is joined in full by the Chief Justice, Justice Thomas, Justice Sotomayor, Justice Kavanaugh, and Justice Barrett. Justice Jackson has filed a dissenting opinion joined in full by Justice Kagan and in part by Justice Gorsuch.
